data.title: As a developer, cleanup recipes run after sub-agent returns and before code review
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Post-implementation cleanup phase that runs applicable cleanup recipes (e.g., normalize-imports) after each sub-agent returns and before code review.
**Why**: Mechanical defects (import ordering, formatting inconsistencies) are caught deterministically before review, reducing reviewer noise and preventing reviewable defects.
**Scope**:
- IN: Post-sub-agent cleanup phase insertion between existing sprint Steps 1c (discoveries) and Step 7 (review), applicable recipe detection from registry, cleanup execution, sprint log records post-cleanup state
- OUT: Recipe task execution (S2), pre-flight (S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, applicable cleanup recipes run after each sub-agent returns and before code review
  ← Satisfies: "A post-implementation cleanup phase runs applicable cleanup recipes after each sub-agent returns and before code review"
- When this story is complete, cleanup recipe fixing import ordering is visible in the sprint log diff before review captures it
  ← Satisfies: "verified by cleanup recipe fixing import ordering before review captures the diff"
- When this story is complete, sprint log records the post-cleanup state (not pre-cleanup) so completion-verifier and reviewers see accurate diffs
  ← Satisfies: sprint log integrity (adversarial review finding)
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Cleanup phase inserts between existing sprint steps — must not disrupt the existing pipeline ordering
- [Correctness] If cleanup produces a no-op (diff is empty after recipe), skip recording to avoid log clutter
- [Correctness] Sub-agent may have already performed the same cleanup as part of LLM-driven implementation — cleanup recipe on already-cleaned code produces no-op, which is harmless, but if the sub-agent made intentional deviations (e.g., specific import order for circular dependency), the deterministic cleanup could revert them. Detect and log conflicts.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
